title: Add background styling to comments for visual distinction
description:
Comments in the feed lack visual separation — they are plain text blocks with only a thin border-bottom between them, making them hard to distinguish from each other and from the surrounding card content.

Add a subtle background (e.g. slightly tinted var(--bg) or a lighter shade) with rounded corners and padding to each .commentItem, so each comment feels like its own distinct block. This gives immediate visual clarity about where one comment ends and another begins.

Reference: CommentSection.module.css .commentItem

title: View all comments button does not show all comments
description:
Bug: Clicking 'View all N comments' hides the button but still only shows the 2 latest comments instead of all N.

Repro steps:
1. Have a feed event with 3+ comments
2. Feed shows 2 latest comments and a 'View all N comments' button
3. Click the button
4. Button disappears but only the same 2 comments remain visible

Root cause likely in CommentSection.tsx: when expanded is set to true, the query fires with initialData set to latestComments (2 most recent). The query may be serving initialData without refetching, or the backend GET /api/social/feed/:eventId/comments endpoint may not be returning all comments.

Reference: CommentSection.tsx lines 44-49 (initialData / enabled logic)

title: Limit reactions to one emoji per user per feed event
description:
Currently users can react with multiple different emojis on the same feed event. Users should only be able to select one emoji reaction per event — selecting a new emoji should replace the previous one, similar to LinkedIn reactions.

Reference: ActionRow.tsx, backend social routes (POST /api/social/feed/:eventId/reactions)

title: Improve comment visual separation in feed
description:
Comments in the social feed are hard to distinguish from each other. Currently they only have a thin border-bottom line between them, with no background differentiation, no avatars/initials, and small similar-colored text that blends together.

Proposed improvements:
- Add colored user initial circles (avatar placeholders) to the left of each comment, using a deterministic color based on username — this provides instant visual anchoring for who said what
- Add a subtle background color to each comment (e.g. var(--bg-card) or a slightly lighter shade) with rounded corners and padding, so each comment feels like its own distinct bubble
- Increase vertical spacing between comments (gap: var(--space-md) instead of var(--space-sm))
- Make the username bolder/slightly larger to create clearer visual hierarchy

Reference: CommentSection.tsx and CommentSection.module.css

title: Facebook-style social feed redesign
Feed event owner cannot read or post comments on their own events
description: POST and GET /api/social/feed/:eventId/comments use getAcceptedFriendIds() which does not include self. The event owner is blocked from commenting on or reading comments on their own events (403). DELETE correctly allows owner to delete. Access control should allow: friends of owner + owner themselves.

title: Include own events in social feed
description: The feed currently only shows friends' activity (userId: { in: friendIds }), excluding the user's own events. This means if someone comments or reacts on your workout, you can't find that feed item anywhere in the UI. Fix: include the current user's ID in the feed query so your own events always appear alongside friends' events. Change the feed query in backend/src/routes/social.ts to include userId in the friendIds array. Also handle the edge case where friendIds is empty — currently returns early with empty array, but should still show own events.

title: Friend request received push notification
description: Send a notification to the user when someone sends them a friend request. Currently only friend request acceptance triggers a notification; the recipient of a new request gets no notification at all.

title: Fix WebKit E2E flakiness on CI
description: E2E tests fail on CI in WebKit: (1) achievements.spec.ts - completion dialog not found after clicking complete button, times out waiting for confirm dialog or Back to Dashboard link. (2) admin-plan-editor.spec.ts - page.goto encounters WebKit internal error. Likely WebKit-specific timing/stability issues in CI environment.

title: Merge username and displayName into a single field
description: Currently User has both username (optional, unique) and displayName (required, not unique). For a workout tracker, a single required unique username field that serves as both the identifier and display name is simpler. Combine them: make username required and unique, remove displayName, use username everywhere displayName was used (UI display, friend lists, etc.). Update registration, settings, friend search, and all API responses.

title: Flaky E2E: isVisible() snapshot check in rest-timer confirmSet loop
description: e2e/rest-timer.spec.ts line 79 uses isVisible() (point-in-time snapshot) inside a loop guard before skipRestButton.click(). If the timer banner appears a few ms after the check, the click is never reached. Pre-existing issue found during ticket 040 review.

title: Fix UTC/local timezone inconsistency in date input max and calendar day comparison
description: The date input max attribute uses .toISOString().slice(0,10) (UTC) while WorkoutCalendar.handleDayClick compares dates in local time. For users east of UTC, after midnight UTC but before midnight local time, the max date could be set to yesterday while the calendar allows clicking today, causing an inconsistency. Both should use the same local-time date string (e.g., toLocaleDateString('en-CA')).

title: ReactionBar: block only the clicked emoji during pending mutation, not all emoji buttons
description: When a mutation is pending, all 5 emoji buttons are disabled via mutation.isPending. Only the tapped emoji should be blocked. Prevents the user from reacting with a different emoji while one is in flight.

title: Real-Time Notifications (SSE + Toast)
PWA bottom navbar touch targets too small — hard to tap
description: The bottom navigation bar in the PWA is too small, making it difficult to tap on mobile devices. Increase the height and touch target size of the bottom navbar to meet the 44px minimum touch target guideline.

title: Push Notifications (Service Worker)
description: Add push notifications via Web Push API so users get notified even when the app is in the background. Requires VAPID key generation, subscription management (store push subscriptions in DB), and service worker push event handler. Builds on top of ticket 080 (SSE + Toast) — reuse the same notification events but deliver via push when user is offline/backgrounded. Scope: VAPID setup, PushSubscription CRUD endpoint, sw.js push handler, opt-in UI. CONSTRAINT: Do not add any npm dependencies — implement everything using built-in Node.js crypto and native Web Push APIs.

title: E2E streak test uses setDate (local) instead of setUTCDate for yesterday calculation — may produce wrong date near midnight in UTC-offset environments
description: In e2e/social.spec.ts line 70, yesterday is computed with setDate(getDate() - 1) which uses local time. The streak calculation in the backend uses UTC. In UTC+N timezones, the local date could be one day ahead of UTC, causing the custom workout to be saved with the wrong UTC date and the streak test to fail near midnight. Should use setUTCDate / getUTCDate like the backend does.

title: streak_milestone and badge_unlocked not emitted for custom workouts
description: The createCustomWorkout() path in workout.service.ts only creates a workout_completed feed event. It does not run the streak/badge logic that the two completeWorkout() paths run. Custom workouts can advance a streak and should trigger the same milestone checks.

title: Comment on friend activity feed events
description: Allow users to comment on their friends' activity feed events (workouts, achievements, streaks, etc). Users should be able to add text comments and view a comment thread on any feed event. Remember notifications

title: streak_milestone deduplication has no DB-level uniqueness guarantee — concurrent completions can create duplicate events
description: The findFirst + create pattern inside the transaction prevents duplicate streak_milestone events under normal sequential execution, but does not protect against two concurrent completeWorkout calls racing in separate transactions for the same user. A unique partial index on (user_id, event_type, payload->>'days') would provide a hard guarantee at the database level.

title: Calendar: selected day indicator does not show date label near Add Custom Workout button
description: After selecting an empty calendar day, the Add Custom Workout button gives no indication of which date was selected. Add a small selected-date label or include the date in the button text for clarity.

title: Calendar improvements: selectable days with custom workout button
description: Users can click/select days in the calendar. After selecting a day, buttons appear below the calendar to add a custom workout for that date.

title: Polish custom workout modal: pre-select clicked calendar day
description: When opening the custom workout modal from a calendar day, the date should be pre-filled with the clicked day so users don't have to pick it again.

title: Custom free-form workout logging
description: Allow users to log ad-hoc gym sessions (not from a plan) directly from the calendar view. User can pick a date, add exercises (from existing exercise list), specify sets with reps and weight for each, and save. These custom workouts appear in the calendar and workout history for later review. Key requirements: (1) Add workout entry point from calendar day tap/click, (2) Exercise picker from existing exercises, (3) Per-set logging: weight (kg) and reps, (4) Save as a new workout type (e.g. status=custom or a flag) so it's distinguishable from plan-generated workouts, (5) View saved custom workouts in history/calendar.

title: Social / Gym Buddy Features
description: Share workouts with friends, compare progress on the same plan, leaderboard, simple feed. Friend system, notifications (e.g. 'Your friend just hit a new Bench TM'). Multi-layer: DB (friend relationships, feed events), API (social endpoints), frontend (feed, profiles, friend management).

title: N+1 queries in workout completion logic
description: The completeWorkout service (workout.service.ts:354-406) runs individual Exercise.findUnique and TrainingMax.findFirst queries inside a loop over progression sets. Should batch-fetch all exercises and current TMs before the loop to avoid N+1.

title: Missing database indexes on foreign keys
description: Several high-query foreign keys lack explicit indexes: TrainingMax needs @@index([userId, exerciseId]) for 'get current TM' queries (the @@unique includes effectiveDate which is less efficient for latest-TM lookups), Workout needs @@index([userId, createdAt]) for calendar/history queries, UserPlan needs @@index([userId]) for subscription lookups. These become important as data grows.

title: Flaky E2E: replace waitForTimeout in rest-timer tests
description: rest-timer.spec.ts lines 87 and 111 use page.waitForTimeout(200) before asserting timer is not visible. This is a classic flakiness pattern. Replace with expect(restTimerBanner).not.toBeVisible() which polls automatically. Also line 98 uses page.locator('#rest-timer-enabled') instead of semantic getByRole locator.

title: Unsafe error handling in frontend catch blocks
description: Multiple frontend files use catch (err: any) and access err.message without type checking: ExerciseFormModal.tsx:76, usePlanEditorState.ts:118, login.tsx:26-28. Should use 'err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err)' pattern consistently. Also api/client.ts line 53 throws raw response body without wrapping in a proper Error - callers have to guess the shape.

title: Inconsistent getUserId() usage in backend routes
description: Some routes use the getUserId(req) helper (which throws if userId is missing) while others access req.userId directly (which is typed as optional). Inconsistent files: users.ts:13,28 and trainingMaxes.ts:28,58 use raw req.userId. All authenticated routes should use getUserId(req) for consistency and runtime safety.

title: Missing DATABASE_URL validation in db.ts
description: backend/src/lib/db.ts passes process.env.DATABASE_URL to PrismaPg without checking if it's defined. If the env var is missing, the error will come from deep inside PrismaPg with a cryptic message. Add an explicit check: if (\!process.env.DATABASE_URL) throw new Error('DATABASE_URL environment variable is required').

title: Update CLAUDE.md: vitest fileParallelism is true not false
description: CLAUDE.md states 'Backend vitest.config.ts uses fileParallelism: false' but the actual config has fileParallelism: true. Either update the docs to match reality, or change the config to false if test isolation requires it. Currently setup.ts only disconnects in afterAll (no table truncation per file), so parallel execution may be intentional.

title: Workout Schedule
description: Assign plan days to specific weekdays (e.g. Day 1 = Monday, Day 2 = Wednesday) and visualize upcoming sessions in the calendar. Set once, referenced often for planning rest days and travel.

title: Progression Charts
description: New /progress page with charts: TM progression lines per lift, AMRAP performance scatter plot, workout frequency heatmap. Time range selector (1M/3M/6M/All). New backend endpoint for AMRAP history. Lightweight chart library (uPlot or similar).

title: Workout History Refinement
description: Improve the workout history view: better readability and visual design, ability to delete old workouts, and overall UI/UX polish. Current history is hard to read and lacks basic management features.

title: Rest Timer
description: Configurable rest timer that auto-starts on set completion with countdown display, adjust/skip controls, and settings for duration.

title: Screen Wake Lock
description: Keep screen on during active workouts using the Screen Wake Lock API. Auto-acquire on workout page mount, release on leave/complete. Re-acquire on visibilitychange for iOS. No UI needed, graceful no-op on unsupported browsers.

title: Dynamic exercise list on Progress page
description: The Progress page currently hardcodes 4 exercises (Bench, Squat, OHP, Deadlift) in EXERCISE_CONFIGS. Make the exercise list dynamic, driven by the active plan's exercises. This way if a user subscribes to a plan with different exercises, the Progress page adapts automatically.

title: Plan switch marker on progression chart
description: When a user switches plans, TM values may not be directly comparable (e.g. different TM percentages). Add a visual vertical marker on the progression chart showing when a plan switch occurred, so users understand context for any jumps or drops in TM values.

title: Custom TM Override
description: Manually adjust a training max with a reason note (e.g. deload reset, injury setback, form correction). Uses existing append-only TM history. Critical when auto-progression overshoots reality.

title: PWA support - installable to home screen
description: Add web app manifest (manifest.json) with app name, icons, theme color, display: standalone. Add minimal service worker for installability. Link manifest in index.html. Goal: be able to Add to Home Screen on iOS Safari and get a standalone app experience.

title: Achievement Badges
description: Unlock badges for workout milestones. Examples: 'Century Club' (100kg lift), 'Iron Streak' (4 weeks no missed days), 'AMRAP King' (+5 reps on progression set), 'Sunrise Grinder' (workout before 7am). Shareable badge cards. Requires defining achievement criteria, detection logic, and a display page.

title: Feed Event Reactions
description: React to friends' feed events with quick emoji reactions (fire, clap, skull, flexed bicep, etc.). Tap-to-react on any feed item. Reactions shown as small emoji row under the event. Low-friction social engagement without typing.

title: Workout Streak Visibility
description: Show friends' current workout streaks on the friends list and in the feed. 'Chris is on a 12-day streak 🔥'. Streaks calculated from consecutive scheduled workout completions. Motivates casual users through social proof.

title: Estimated 1RM Leaderboard
description: Leaderboard based on calculated e1RM from AMRAP sets using Epley formula (weight * (1 + reps/30)), not just static TMs. More dynamic and rewards actual performance. Could be a tab or toggle on the existing leaderboard.

title: Richer Feed Event Types
description: Expand feed beyond workout_completed and tm_increased. New event types: streak_milestone (7/14/30/60/90 day streaks), badge_unlocked (from Achievement Badges #027), plan_switched, workout_note_shared. Makes the feed more varied and engaging.

title: Find Friends by Username
description: Add optional username field to user profile (unique, alphanumeric + underscores). Allow sending friend requests by username instead of email. More natural for gym acquaintances where you wouldn't share email. Search with autocomplete.

title: Rename app from nSuns to SetForge
description: Rename all user-facing surfaces: HTML title, PWA manifest, Layout header, SW cache name, E2E test assertions, and CLAUDE.md references

title: Update POST /api/workouts/:id/complete docs to mention newAchievements in response
description: The docs/api-endpoints.md entry for POST /api/workouts/:id/complete still says 'returns { progressions: [...] }'. It should be updated to include newAchievements: [...] in the documented response shape after ticket 027 added achievements.

title: Fix E2E streak test to use setUTCDate for yesterday calculation
description: E2E streak test uses setDate/getDate (local time) for yesterday but toISOString() returns UTC. Near midnight in UTC-offset timezones this produces the wrong date, causing the 2 day streak assertion to fail.

title: docs/db-schema.md feed_events event_type list is missing streak_milestone, badge_unlocked, and plan_switched
description: The db-schema.md feed_events documentation only lists workout_completed and tm_increased as event_type values. Three new event types added in ticket 065 (streak_milestone, badge_unlocked, plan_switched) are not reflected in the doc. Should be updated per CLAUDE.md documentation requirements.

title: workout.service.ts push calls for workout_completed and badge_unlocked are missing url field — navigation from those self-push notifications lands on / instead of /social/feed or /achievements
description: The workout.service.ts completeWorkout and createCustomWorkout functions call pushService.sendToUser with workout_completed and badge_unlocked payloads that lack a url field. The notificationclick handler in sw.js falls back to / for missing urls. Only the NEW calls added to workouts.ts route handler include the url field. The existing service-layer calls, which fire first for self-notifications, do not.

title: Push notification click navigation
description:
When a push notification is tapped, navigate the user to the relevant view in the app. Implementation:

1. **Backend**: Add a `url` field to each push notification payload (include `feedEventId` in comment_received payload for deep-linking):
   - `comment_received` → `/social/feed?event=<feedEventId>` (scroll to and highlight the specific feed event)
   - `friend_request_received` → `/social/friends`
   - `friend_request_accepted` → `/social/friends`
   - `workout_completed` → `/social/feed`
   - `badge_unlocked` → `/achievements`

2. **Service worker (sw.js)**: Add a `notificationclick` event handler that:
   - Closes the notification
   - Reads `data.url` from the notification
   - If an app window is already open, navigates it and focuses it
   - Otherwise opens a new window at that URL

3. **Frontend social page**: Convert tabs to proper sub-routes:
   - Create route files: `social/feed.tsx`, `social/friends.tsx`, `social/leaderboard.tsx`
   - `/social` redirects to `/social/feed` (default tab)
   - Tab navigation uses `<Link>` / `<ButtonLink>` for proper anchor tags
   - `social/feed.tsx` reads optional `?event=<id>` search param via `validateSearch`
   - When `event` search param is present, scroll to that feed event and highlight it (e.g. brief CSS animation)

Bump service worker cache name to `setforge-v3` since behavior changes.

title: Frontend UI pattern hooks: useDialog and shared validation
description: Extract recurring frontend UI patterns into reusable hooks and shared modules: (1) useDialog(open, onClose) hook to replace the duplicated dialog lifecycle useEffect pairs (showModal/close sync + close event listener) found in CustomWorkoutModal.tsx, workout page achievement dialog, and future modals. (2) Shared validation module (validators.ts) for username validation — currently defined separately in register.tsx and settings.tsx with slightly different behavior. Move the regex and rules to one place, parameterize required-vs-optional. Also covers the backend username regex in auth.ts, users.ts, social.ts (could share via a common package or just align).

title: Refactor workout.service.ts: extract streak/badge helper and STREAK_MILESTONES constant
description:
Two small refactors in workout.service.ts:

1. The streak + badge feed event emission block (~40 lines) is duplicated in both plan and no-plan completeWorkout transaction paths. Extract to a shared helper function.

2. The array [7, 14, 30, 60, 90] is defined inline at three separate locations (lines 530, 614, 809). Extract to a single module-level STREAK_MILESTONES constant.

title: Docs catchup: update api-endpoints.md, db-schema.md, and react-query-cache.md
description:
Multiple doc files are stale. All updates in one pass:

1. api-endpoints.md: add GET /api/social/search, updated POST /api/social/request (from ticket 068). Add POST/GET/DELETE /api/social/feed/:eventId/comments and commentCount + latestComments fields in GET /api/social/feed response. Add comment_received notification type.

2. db-schema.md: add username column in users table. Add push_subscriptions table (id, user_id FK, endpoint TEXT, p256dh TEXT, auth TEXT, created_at; unique user_id+endpoint).

3. react-query-cache.md: add social/search query key. Update stale references from ReactionBar.tsx to ActionRow.tsx, and from 'CommentSection (inside CommentModal)' to CommentSection.tsx.

title: Calendar: clear selectedDateKey after deletion and custom workout save
description:
Two places in history.tsx fail to clear selectedDateKey:

1. After workout deletion: calendar refetches, deleted day has no workouts, showAddCustomWorkoutButton becomes true and button appears without user interaction. Fix: call setSelectedDateKey(null) in handleConfirmDelete.

2. After custom workout save: the .selected highlight on the calendar day persists until the user clicks another day or changes month. Fix: call setSelectedDateKey(null) in handleCustomWorkoutSaved.

title: Custom workout small fixes: calendar badge shows 0, no error for future date
description:
Two small custom workout bugs:

1. Calendar badge shows 0: In WorkoutCalendar.tsx:219, badge displays dayNumber instead of count. Custom workouts have dayNumber: 0, so badge renders 0 instead of 1. Fix: day.workouts[0]!.dayNumber || day.workouts.length.

2. No error for future date: In CustomWorkoutModal, manually entering a future date gets native HTML5 invalid state but no visible error message. Save button silently stays disabled. Fix: add inline validation text 'Date cannot be in the future' when date input is invalid.

title: ActionRow React button shows same 'React' label in both active and inactive state
description: The button text reads 'React' whether the user has already reacted or not. When active it should say something like 'Remove' or 'Reacted' so the label conveys the action that will happen on press.

title: Backend feed endpoint fetches ALL comments unbounded — add LIMIT or take-last-2 in DB query instead of in-memory slice
description: GET /api/social/feed batch-fetches ALL comments for up to 20 events with no LIMIT. In the worst case this could be thousands of rows. The backend should instead use a ranked subquery or window function to fetch only the 2 most recent comments per event at the DB level, not slice in memory.

title: SocialContent.tsx component is now a dead code orphan — social route was refactored to sub-routes but SocialContent was not deleted
description: After converting social.tsx to a layout route with sub-routes in ticket 108, the SocialContent component (frontend/src/components/SocialContent.tsx) is no longer imported by any route. It remains in the codebase as dead code. The social.tsx route layout now imports SocialContent.module.css directly. SocialContent.tsx itself can be deleted.

title: auth.ts email-exists P2002 guard uses raw object check instead of isPrismaError
description: In auth.ts POST /register, the username-exists branch uses isP2002UsernameViolation() but the email-exists fallthrough at line 35 still does a raw object check (err.code === P2002). Should use isPrismaError(err, P2002) for consistency.

title: auth.ts: email-exists P2002 guard still uses raw object check instead of isPrismaError()
description: The username-exists branch in auth.ts register handler correctly uses isP2002UsernameViolation, but the fallback email-exists branch (line 35) uses a hand-rolled check (err && 'code' in err && err.code === 'P2002') instead of the new isPrismaError() helper. Should be replaced for consistency.

title: Fix ApiError to preserve error.code from server responses
description: Currently apiFetch discards the code field when constructing ApiError, making code-based error handling impossible in UI (e.g. USERNAME_EXISTS check in settings.tsx)

title: Fix flaky social test: 'no longer shows in pending requests after acceptance' gets 404 intermittently
description: The test at social.test.ts:165 calls GET /api/social/requests but intermittently receives 404 instead of 200. The route exists and has no 404 code path, suggesting a race condition or Express 5 routing issue. All ticket 117 changes are frontend-only — this is a pre-existing backend test fragility.

title: Replace TM progress with e1RM from completed sets
description: Replace TM-based progress chart with e1RM computed from completed sets (Epley formula). Program-agnostic progression tracking. Design: docs/plans/2026-03-08-e1rm-progression-design.md. Plan: docs/plans/2026-03-08-e1rm-progression-plan.md

title: Add security middleware: helmet, rate limiting, CORS origin, body limit, trust proxy
description: backend/src/app.ts uses wide-open cors(), no helmet, no rate limiting on auth (login does real bcrypt = brute-force + CPU DoS), no explicit JSON body limit, no trust proxy for Traefik. Add helmet, express-rate-limit on auth, CORS_ORIGIN gating, body limit, and trust proxy in production.

title: Workout page hangs on Loading when starting a fresh workout (StrictMode + cancel-flag drops creation result)
description: Starting a new workout (loader returns type:none) creates it server-side (POST /api/workouts 201) but the page stays stuck on 'Loading' until a manual reload. Root cause in workout.$dayNumber.tsx: the create effect's createRef guard prevents StrictMode's second invoke from re-firing, while the first invoke's cleanup sets cancelled=true, so the resolved startWorkout result is discarded. Manifests in dev (StrictMode double-invoke); prod build is unaffected, which is why E2E against the prod image passed. Fixed by dropping the per-run cancelled flag (setState after unmount is a no-op in React 18+).

title: Graceful shutdown on SIGTERM/SIGINT with Prisma disconnect
description: backend/src/index.ts only handles unhandledRejection/uncaughtException. Coolify/Traefik send SIGTERM on every redeploy, killing in-flight requests and leaking DB connections. Add a handler: server.close() then prisma disconnect.

title: Harden production Dockerfile: non-root USER, HEALTHCHECK, .dockerignore
description: Container runs as root (Dockerfile stage 3 never sets USER). Add the built-in node user + chown + USER node, a HEALTHCHECK hitting /api/health, and a .dockerignore (excludes node_modules, dist, .git, logs, playwright-report). Container-escape hardening and faster builds for Coolify.

title: Fix vulnerable production dependencies + add npm audit CI gate
description: npm audit --omit=dev reports 14 vulns (9 high), incl. path-to-regexp ReDoS and qs DoS (transitive via Express 5). No audit step in CI. Run npm audit fix and add an audit gate to the deploy workflow.

title: Precache app shell via vite-plugin-pwa for offline reliability
description: public/sw.js only cache.add('/') on install; hashed assets are cached opportunistically only. A cold load or reload on flaky gym wifi fails to boot. Adopt vite-plugin-pwa (Workbox) to precache the built asset manifest with an offline fallback.

title: Persist/queue active-workout set logging for offline resilience
description: sw.js passes all api requests through with no caching and there is no React Query persistence. If wifi drops mid-set, logged reps fail silently and data is lost. Add a write queue / background-sync or localStorage-backed mutation retry for set logging.

title: Add root error boundary with branded recovery screen
description: __root.tsx has no errorComponent; only 3 leaf routes do. An uncaught render error elsewhere shows TanStack's bare default white screen. Add a root errorComponent with a branded reload screen.

title: Respect safe-area-inset-top on header for notch/standalone
description: Layout.tsx header uses fixed padding with no safe-area-inset-top, and status-bar-style is default. In installed standalone mode the header crowds the iPhone notch. Add safe-area-inset-top padding and revisit status-bar-style.

title: Mobile input hints on auth forms (inputMode, autoComplete)
description: Login/Register email inputs lack inputMode=email, autoComplete, autoCapitalize=none; password fields lack autoComplete current-password/new-password. Mobile keyboards capitalize emails and password managers do not autofill on the most-used cold-start screen.

title: App-like tap polish: tap-highlight-color, user-select, overscroll-behavior
description: No global tap-highlight-color, touch-callout, or user-select none on buttons; tapping flashes grey iOS highlight and long-press selects text. No overscroll-behavior-y contain, so pull-to-refresh can accidentally reload. Add these for native feel.

title: Flaky/tz-dependent: progress plan-switch marker missing when switch is same-day as latest workout (fails after ~21:00 UTC in UTC+3)
description: e2e/progress.spec.ts:100 'shows plan switch marker on chart after switching plans' fails deterministically when the suite runs late enough in the UTC day. Root cause: ProgressChart marker normalizes the switch date to LOCAL midnight (d.setHours(0,0,0,0)) while data-point timestamps (dates = new Date(d.date).getTime()) are NOT normalized. The plan switch occurs chronologically just after the last workout data point, so its timestamp is > maxDate; the local-midnight snap only rescues it while the switch's local day still equals the last workout's day. In UTC+3, a switch at >=21:00 UTC snaps to the NEXT local day (Jun 25), pushing ts past maxDate so 'if (ts < minDate || ts > maxDate) return []' drops the marker. Verified: fails on clean main (without unrelated UX changes) only after 21:00 UTC. Fix: use consistent UTC-based date handling for both data points and markers (or clamp an in-range-by-day switch to maxDate), and make the test robust to timezone/time-of-day. Related to flaky-tests ticket 090.

title: Route-based code splitting (autoCodeSplitting)
description: vite.config.ts tanstackRouter() lacks autoCodeSplitting and there are no lazy routes, so all ~27 route components (admin, social, charts) ship in one bundle. The login screen downloads everything. Enable autoCodeSplitting and/or manualChunks.

title: PWA install prompt (Add to Home Screen affordance)
description: Manifest is installable but there is no beforeinstallprompt capture or UI nudging install. The offline + wake-lock benefits only apply once installed, and most users will not discover it. Add a dismissible install prompt.

title: Change password
description: No endpoint or UI to change a password (auth.service.ts has only register/login/refresh). A logged-in user can never rotate their password. Table-stakes account security.

title: Delete account (GDPR + app-store requirement)
description: No DELETE /users/me and no UI. Users cannot remove their account or data, a legal and app-store-review requirement. Add account deletion with data cleanup.

title: Personal data export (GDPR portability)
description: No export endpoint. Serious lifters expect to download training history (CSV/JSON) and portability is a GDPR right. Add an export of workouts, TMs, and progress.

title: Warm-up set calculation
description: No warm-up logic anywhere; generated workouts jump straight to working sets at high percentages. Mature percentage-based apps auto-generate a warm-up ramp (e.g. 40/50/60% of working weight). Core to safe execution.

title: New-user onboarding flow
description: After register the user is dropped into a bare TM-entry form with no welcome, plan-selection guidance, or explanation of what a 1RM is. Add a guided first-run onboarding flow.

title: Configure Prisma connection pool limit
description: backend/src/lib/db.ts builds PrismaPg with only a connection string, no pool size. Under Coolify shared Postgres an unbounded pool can exhaust connections. Set an explicit connection_limit sized to the deployment.

title: Edit past/completed workout sets
description: Set logging and PATCH sets only work on in-progress workouts; history detail is read-only. Users need to fix a mistyped rep/weight after finishing, otherwise bad data corrupts e1RM/progression analytics. Allow editing completed workouts.

title: Flaky tests: workout-feed-events 404 and admin-plan-editor timeout
description:
Two flaky tests to investigate:

1. workout-feed-events.test.ts: occasionally fails with 404 on POST /api/workouts during parallel test execution (likely a race condition in test setup). Second run passes.

2. admin-plan-editor E2E: unsaved changes warning test times out on Create Account button click in admin setup phase (webkit, intermittent).

title: Flaky: plans.test.ts 'GET /api/plans/:id requires authentication' intermittently 404 instead of 401
description: Backend test src/__tests__/routes/plans.test.ts > 'GET /api/plans/:id requires authentication' intermittently asserts 'expected 404 to be 401' (passed on re-run). Likely test isolation/route-ordering: an unauthenticated GET /api/plans/:id should hit the authenticate middleware (401) but occasionally returns 404. Investigate whether a route or middleware ordering lets some requests reach a 404 path before auth, or whether parallel-test state causes it. Related to flaky-tests ticket 090.

title: Flaky backend tests: recurring training_maxes_exercise_id_fkey FK violation + social leaderboard undefined.id
description: Several backend test runs this session intermittently fail with a Prisma FK violation on training_maxes_exercise_id_fkey (POST /api/training-maxes/setup referencing an exerciseId not present), and social.test.ts > Leaderboard 'Cannot read properties of undefined (reading id)'. Both point to test-isolation/DB-state issues — exercises or expected setup data sometimes missing when a test runs (possibly parallel test files sharing the DB, or a test deleting/relying on seed data). Investigate test isolation for the exercises table and the leaderboard test's setup. Related to flaky-tests tickets 090, and the plans-auth flake.

title: Plate Calculator
description: Tap weight on any set to see plate breakdown per side. Greedy algorithm from heaviest plate down. Configurable bar weight and available plates in Settings. Frontend-only, no backend changes.

title: Dark Mode
description: System-aware dark mode following device preference, with manual toggle in Settings (System/Light/Dark). Redefine existing CSS custom properties under data-theme attribute. Store preference in localStorage. Instant switch, no reload.

title: PR Detection
description: Detect personal records in real-time during workouts. PR = more weight at same or higher rep count than ever before for that exercise. Show animated celebration banner on AMRAP completion. New backend endpoint for per-exercise records by rep count.

title: Personal Records board on Progress (best e1RM per lift)
description: DONE (shipped). GET /api/progress/records returns the single best estimated-1RM set per exercise (strongest first; earliest date on ties); a 'Personal Records' section at the top of the Progress page shows each lift's est. 1RM with the producing weight x reps and date (UTC). This is the read-only foundation extracted from ticket 008 (PR Detection). 008 remains for the richer real-time scope: detecting a new PR mid-workout, an animated celebration banner on AMRAP completion, and per-rep-count records.

title: Gym Crew / Groups
description: Create a named group of friends (a 'crew') that shares its own leaderboard and activity feed. Good for real-life gym groups or online communities running the same plan. Crew has a name, members, shared leaderboard, and crew feed.

title: Workout Duration
description: Track and display how long each workout took. Show duration badge on history calendar entries. Helps users plan gym visits around their schedule.

title: Bodyweight Tracking
description: Log bodyweight (daily or per-session) with a trend line chart. Casual users want weekly weigh-ins to see if training is working. Power users want daily logging, strength-to-bodyweight ratios, and correlation with TM progression.

title: Workout Progress Indicator
description: Progress bar and set counter at top of workout page showing 'X / 17 sets completed'. Bar fills as sets complete, color transitions gray to blue to green. Frontend-only, derived from existing set data.

title: Workout Notes
description: Optional free-text notes on workouts. 'Add note' link on workout page opens textarea (max 500 chars), auto-saves on blur. Notes shown in history detail view. Requires new DB column and API update.

title: Fix push notifications: VAPID key format, subscribe schema mismatch, and button touch targets
description:
Push subscribe is broken in real browsers due to multiple issues. Fix all together:

1. VAPID public key format: GET /api/notifications/public-key returns SPKI DER base64url (91 bytes) but PushManager.subscribe() requires raw EC point (65 bytes, 04||x||y). Real browsers throw InvalidAccessError. Fix: return raw EC point from the endpoint, or convert in frontend.

2. Subscribe response schema mismatch: pushSubscribeResponseSchema expects { id, userId, endpoint, createdAt } but backend POST /subscribe returns { ok: true }. Causes ZodError on successful 201, showing 'Failed to enable notifications'. Fix: align schema to match actual API response.

3. Button touch targets: PushNotificationSettings.module.css .actionBtn and .actionBtnSecondary use min-height 2.75rem instead of project-standard 3rem. Update to 3rem.

title: Change account email
description: PATCH /users/me accepts only username; the email login identifier can never be changed. Add email change with re-verification once email infra exists.

title: Dashboard stats: workouts-this-week + streak
description: Surface the user's own training momentum on the dashboard: GET /api/workouts/stats returns currentStreak (consecutive-day, reusing calculateStreak) and workoutsLast7Days; a DashboardStats component shows 'N this week' and a '🔥 N day streak' when >=2. The day-streak was previously only computed for achievements and shown for friends, never to the user themselves.

title: Repoint CI/CD from Railway to Coolify deploy
description: deploy.yml and deploy.sh trigger a Railway redeploy via Railway GraphQL API. Production target is Coolify, which deploys via webhook or registry/git polling. Repoint the pipeline at Coolify (deploy webhook + token) or configure Coolify to pull the ghcr.io image.

title: Document required production env vars for Coolify
description: Backend requires JWT_SECRET, DATABASE_URL, VAPID_PUBLIC_KEY, VAPID_PRIVATE_KEY (optional LOG_LEVEL/PORT) via requireEnv. No .env.example or doc lists them, so a Coolify deployer cannot know what secrets to inject. Add a documented required-vars list.

title: PR Celebration Post
description: When a user hits a personal record (requires PR Detection #008), auto-generate a celebratory feed event with confetti animation. Shows the lift, weight, reps, and how it compares to previous best. Friends see it prominently in their feed.

title: Recent Workout Peek
description: Before starting a workout, show a summary of last session for that day: key weights and reps. Helps users mentally prepare and know what to expect.

title: Validate URL params and query strings with Zod
description: Body validation is solid but params/query have gaps: (1) PATCH/DELETE /api/admin/exercises/:id have no isNaN check causing 500s, (2) All :id params allow 0 or negative values, (3) GET /api/workouts/history limit has no upper bound, (4) GET /api/workouts/calendar year has no range bounds, (5) PATCH /api/workouts/:id/sets/:setId never validates :id param. Fix: extend validate middleware or add Zod schemas for params/query on all routes.

title: Accessibility: fix color-contrast (primary), add meta description + robots.txt
description: Lighthouse mobile audit (dashboard): Accessibility 94, Best Practices 100, SEO 82. Failures: (1) color-contrast — white text on --primary #3b82f6 = 3.67 (need 4.5) on all primary buttons; active bottom-nav link (primary on dark) = 4.19. (2) SEO meta-description missing. (3) robots.txt invalid. Fix primary contrast (darken for button bg + lighter accent for nav-text-on-dark), add meta description + robots.txt.

title: Previous performance reference during a workout
description: On the active workout page, show each exercise's last-time performance ('Last time: 80 kg × 8') so the user knows what to beat (progressive overload). Backend GET /workouts/:id/previous returns a map exerciseName -> { weight, reps, completedAt } from the AMRAP/top set of the most recent OTHER completed workout containing that exercise. Frontend shows it under each exercise header in ActiveWorkoutView.

title: Effort / RPE Logging
description: Log perceived effort after workouts or per-set. Simple mode: post-workout Easy/Good/Hard rating. Advanced mode: per-set RPE 1-10 scale. Track trends over time to detect under-recovery or peaking.

title: User Profile Page
description: Public-ish profile page showing: display name, member since date, current plan, lift stats summary (current TMs or e1RMs), workout streak, badges earned, total workouts completed. Viewable by friends. Foundation for social identity in the app.

title: Normalize email casing across register/login/changeEmail
description: From ticket 147 review (pre-existing, out of scope there). The email @unique column is case-sensitive Postgres text, and register/login/changeEmail store & match email as-is (no lowercase). So 'User@x.com' and 'user@x.com' can coexist as separate accounts and login is case-sensitive. Normalize email to trimmed-lowercase consistently in register, login lookup, and changeEmail (and consider a one-off data migration / case-insensitive unique index) so accounts are unambiguous.

title: Stabilize timing-sensitive E2E tests under machine load
description: Observed today: under heavy machine load the E2E suite intermittently flakes (different unrelated test each run) on 10s timeouts — page.goto, page.waitForURL (admin fixture), page.waitForResponse (workout.spec previous-performance, flaked twice). retry:2 doesn't always save them. Backend + auth flows always pass. Consider: longer/explicit timeouts on the waitForResponse-based tests, more robust waits (waitForResponse before the action via Promise.all), or reducing parallelism. Relates to the run sometimes taking 16m vs ~1m.

title: Surface today's scheduled workout on the dashboard
description: Users assign training days to weekdays (schedule, in settings) but the dashboard ignores it. Add a prominent 'Today' card at the top of the dashboard that, when today's weekday is scheduled, shows that day's number + exercises + a Start/Continue button. Renders nothing if no schedule or today isn't scheduled (avoids false rest-day claims on partial schedules).

title: Deload Detection
description: Track AMRAP trends and suggest deload when user hits 0-1 reps on progression AMRAP for 3 consecutive workouts. Suggest 10% TM reduction with one-tap apply. New backend stall-check endpoint, no new DB tables needed.

title: Remove dead/broken getWorkoutHistory frontend client
description: From ticket 037 review. frontend/src/api/workouts.ts getWorkoutHistory has ZERO callers and is already broken independent of 037: it treats the response as an array ((data as unknown[]).map) but GET /workouts/history returns { workouts, total, page, limit }. It would throw if ever called. Remove it (or fix its response handling) — the history page uses the calendar endpoints, not this.

title: Migrate calendar query validation to parseQuery (consistency)
description: From ticket 037 review. GET /workouts/calendar still validates year/month with manual parseInt+isNaN+range (code INVALID_PARAMS). Now that parseQuery(schema, req, res) exists, migrate calendar to it for a single consistent query-validation idiom (currently three: VALIDATION_ERROR via parseQuery, INVALID_PARAMS manual on calendar, QUERY_TOO_LONG manual on search). Low priority consistency cleanup.

title: Fix admin plan editor load for category-based progression rules
description: PlanWithDetailsSchema at frontend/src/api/schemas.ts:229 declares 'exercise: ExerciseSchema.optional()' which rejects null. Backend returns exercise:null for category (upper/lower) progression rules, so getAdminPlan's zod parse throws 'expected object, received null' and the edit page shows an error banner with 'No progression rules defined'. Data is unaffected (rules persist correctly). Fix: change to ExerciseSchema.nullable().optional(). Repro: open /admin/plans/<id> for any plan with a category-based progression rule (e.g. Arnold's Golden Six, plan id 4).

title: Plan editor: sticky Save bar overlaps Edit Sets button
description: The sticky 'Save Plan' bar (.stickySaveBar, position:sticky bottom:0) floats over page content. On short days (e.g. one exercise) the 'Edit Sets' button sits at the fold under the bar and can't be scrolled clear, making it hard/impossible to click. Fix: add bottom scroll buffer (padding-bottom) to .page so any element can be scrolled above the pinned bar.

title: Plan editor: duplicate-day / copy exercises from another day
description: Plans with identical days (e.g. full-body 3x/week) require rebuilding every exercise+set scheme per day. There's 'Copy sets from...' within a day but no way to copy a whole day. Add a 'Copy exercises from Day X' control shown when the current day is empty and another day has exercises; deep-clones exercises (new tempIds, reset sortOrder) into the active day.

title: Plan editor: slug generation mangles apostrophes
description: generateSlug() in usePlanEditorState.ts turns 'Arnold's Golden Six' into 'arnold-s-golden-six' because the apostrophe is replaced by a dash along with spaces. Strip apostrophes (straight and curly) before the non-alphanumeric replacement so it becomes 'arnolds-golden-six'.

title: Offline set-log queue: also flush on visibilitychange (iOS Safari online event is unreliable)
description: iOS Safari fires the 'online' event on interface changes, not real reachability, so flush-on-reconnect can lag. Also flush the set-log queue on document visibilitychange->visible. Minor; from ticket 136 review (M4).

title: Feed: custom workout shows 'completed Day 0'
description: BUG (low-medium, cosmetic but confusing). A workout logged via History 'Add Custom Workout' generates a feed event reading 'qa_alpha completed Day 0'. 'Day 0' is wrong/confusing for a non-plan-day custom workout. Expected: name the exercise(s) or say 'completed a custom workout'. Repro: History -> select empty past day -> Add Custom Workout -> save -> Social feed. Found via manual QA, mobile.

title: Inconsistent estimated-1RM for same lift across Progress, Profile modal, and Leaderboard
description: BUG/inconsistency (medium, verify). The same user's same lift shows three different e1RM values across surfaces. Observed for Squat: Progress 'Personal Records' = 92.5 kg; Friend profile modal (Top lifts) = 117.5 kg; Leaderboard Est.1RM = 97.5 kg. Likely different source sets (custom workout incl/excl) and/or different formulas per surface. Decide a single source-of-truth/definition for e1RM and apply consistently, or clearly label what each surface measures. Found via manual QA, mobile.

title: Achievements: unlocked date format (DD/MM/YYYY) inconsistent with rest of app
description: UX (low). Achievements page shows 'Unlocked 28/06/2026' (DD/MM/YYYY) while History/Progress use 'Jun 28, 2026'. Standardize date formatting across the app. Found via manual QA, mobile.

title: Select Plan page does not indicate the user's current/active plan
description: UX (low). On /select-plan when the user already has an active plan, both plan cards show an identical 'Select Plan' button with no marker of which plan is current. Add a 'Current plan' badge / disabled state for the active plan. Found via manual QA, mobile.

title: Missing negative test for century-club achievement (low TM should not unlock)
description: The achievements test suite has a positive test for century-club (bench TM=200) but no negative test confirming it does NOT trigger when all set weights are below 100 kg. The default benchTM=100 users (TM=90) could serve as a negative baseline.

title: Cap and validate history pagination limit
description: workouts.ts history: limit = parseInt(query.limit) || 10 passed straight to Prisma take with no max cap and no negative validation; limit=9999999 loads all workouts+sets (DoS), page=-1 yields negative skip. Cap at ~50 and validate page/limit.

title: validateQuery middleware for query params
description: middleware/validate.ts only safeParses req.body, leaving query params unvalidated app-wide (leaderboard mode, history page/limit). Add a validateQuery variant and apply to query-driven endpoints.

title: Invalidate existing sessions on password change (tokenVersion in JWT)
description: Password change (142) re-hashes the password but does NOT revoke already-issued tokens: this is stateless JWT with a 24h access + 30d refresh token and no token version/denylist, so an attacker's or lost-device's tokens stay valid up to 30 days after the user changes their password. Add a tokenVersion (or passwordChangedAt) column to User, embed it in access+refresh tokens, verify it in the auth/refresh middleware, and bump it on password change (and ideally on logout-all). This is what makes 'change password' actually able to revoke compromised sessions. Surfaced in ticket 142 review (Important #1).

title: Add DB indexes on hot query columns (WorkoutSet.exerciseId)
description: WorkoutSet.exerciseId is unindexed but e1RM leaderboard and progress filter workout_sets by exerciseId IN (...). These are the heaviest read paths and will table-scan as data grows. Add targeted indexes.

title: Rate-limit PATCH /users/me/password (current-password check is unthrottled)
description: The auth rate limiter (131) covers /api/auth/* but not /api/users/me/password. An attacker needs a valid victim JWT to reach the bcrypt current-password check, so exposure is low, but if a token leaks the current-password guess is unthrottled. Apply a rate limiter to this endpoint. Minor; from ticket 142 review.

title: Clear React Query cache (queryClient.clear) on logout/account-delete
description: logout() clears tokens + the offline set-log queue but not the React Query cache, so on a shared device stale cached workout/feed data could linger in memory until consumers unmount. Call queryClient.clear() on logout (and account deletion) for a clean teardown. Low severity (memory only, cleared on reload). From ticket 144 review (Minor #4).

title: Add feed latestComments edge-case tests: exactly 1 comment and zero-event feed
description: The social.test.ts latestComments suite covers 0-comment and 3-comment (truncated to 2) cases but not: (a) exactly 1 comment on an event, (b) the eventIds-empty early-exit branch when the feed returns zero events. Both are handled correctly by the implementation but lack explicit test coverage.

title: docs: DELETE /social/feed/:eventId/comments/:commentId returns 200 with body but docs say 204
description: social.ts line 769 returns res.json({ id: commentId }) with default 200, but api-endpoints.md line 52 documents the response as 204. Fix the doc to say 200 with body { id: number }.

title: routeHelpers.ts: isP2002UsernameViolation uses weaker typeof check vs isPrismaError instanceof Error guard — align both helpers to the same standard
description: isP2002UsernameViolation checks typeof err === 'object' while isPrismaError checks instanceof Error. Both live in routeHelpers.ts. The inconsistency was surfaced by ticket 119 (the two helpers are now used side-by-side in auth.ts). Update isP2002UsernameViolation to use instanceof Error so both helpers apply the same type narrowing.

title: Plate calculator: clearer message when target weight < bar weight
description: From ticket 004 review (minor). computePlatesPerSide returns empty groups for both weight==bar and weight<bar, so a target lighter than the selected bar shows 'Just the bar — no plates needed', which is misleading (that bar can't make the lighter weight). Distinguish perSide<0 from perSide===0 and show a message like 'Lighter than the selected bar'. Low frequency (main-lift prescribed weights rarely dip below 20kg).

title: Bound the stall-detection query (getStalls loads all progression sets)
description: From ticket 009 review (minor, deferred). getStalls fetches every completed isProgression set for the user, then keeps only the newest 3 per exercise in JS. For a multi-year user that's hundreds of rows to use ~12. Bound it with a per-exercise window/limit or a smarter query. Acceptable for now (bounded by user history).
